skill.name: docs-creator
skill.description: Create and organize public documentation pages using Fumadocs. Use when building new documentation pages or organizing existing ones.
skill.summary: Create and organize public documentation pages using Fumadocs. Use when building new documentation p...
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, hardcoded URLs, and weak cryptographic patterns. Review found these are Markdown formatting, documentation examples, and external reference links, with no executable code, no credential access, and no data exfiltration behavior. No prompt injection text or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated inline code spans and fenced Markdown examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines document paths, file names, frontmatter, meta.json, and MDX examples; they are not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown documentation and fenced examples, not scripts or code invoked by the skill. No shell command runner or dynamic execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to public Fumadocs and Lucide documentation. They are reference links in Markdown and do not cause the skill to make network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are embedded in Markdown links and examples only. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, client, or other network execution mechanism.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain documentation metadata fields such as description strings and page frontmatter. No hashing, encryption, digest generation, or cryptographic algorithm usage appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found descriptive documentation text only. There is no cryptographic API call or security-sensitive data transformation.

content.value_statement: Documentation projects need consistent page structure, metadata, navigation, and examples. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create Fumadocs MDX pages with organized folders and meta.json files.
actual_capabilities: Creates MDX documentation pages under src/content/docs.
Adds frontmatter with title, description, and Lucide icon names.
Organizes nested folders to match documentation URL paths.
Creates meta.json files for sidebar order, section titles, icons, and tabs.
Uses Fumadocs UI components such as callouts, cards, tabs, and steps.
Builds relative links between documentation pages.
limitations: It only documents Fumadocs-style content structures shown in the skill files.
It does not validate whether a selected Lucide icon exists.
It does not run the documentation site or check rendered output.
It does not define authentication, API behavior, or application code.

use_cases.title: Create a New Product Guide
use_cases.description: Build a starter documentation section with an introduction page, setup page, and ordered sidebar.

use_cases.title: Organize API Documentation
use_cases.description: Create nested API pages and a meta.json file that groups endpoints, authentication, and examples.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Docs Navigation
use_cases.description: Restructure existing MDX pages into clearer folders, sidebar entries, and section tabs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one new documentation page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Fumadocs MDX page for an introduction topic. Include frontmatter, a clear heading, and relative links to setup and configuration pages.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Documentation Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a folder with ordered navigation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new docs section for installation. Add index, requirements, and configuration pages, plus a meta.json file with sidebar order and an icon.

prompt_templates.title: Build API Reference Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for structured API documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an API reference section with authentication, endpoints, and error handling pages. Use Fumadocs components for steps, callouts, and tabs.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for reorganizing a larger docs tree.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the current src/content/docs structure. Propose a clearer folder plan, then update pages and meta.json files for better navigation.

output_examples.input: Create an introduction page for a platform.
output_examples.output: A new introduction MDX page with frontmatter, a welcome heading, a short overview, and links to installation and configuration.

output_examples.input: Organize API docs into a sidebar section.
output_examples.output: A grouped API folder with pages for introduction, endpoints, and authentication, plus ordered sidebar metadata.
A setup page that uses step-by-step Fumadocs components and includes an informational callout for important requirements.

best_practices: Keep each page focused on one topic and link to related pages with relative links.
Use clear frontmatter descriptions because they support search and previews.
Maintain meta.json files whenever folders or sidebar order changes.
anti_patterns: Do not place unrelated pages in one large flat folder.
Do not use decorative icon names unless they clearly match the page topic.
Do not create navigation entries that do not match existing files or folders.

faq.question: What documentation framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Fumadocs projects that store MDX pages under src/content/docs.

faq.question: Can it create sidebar navigation?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes meta.json files for sidebar order, folder titles, icons, separators, and root sections.

faq.question: Does it support MDX components?
faq.answer: Yes. The reference mentions Fumadocs tabs, steps, and callout components.

faq.question: Can it choose icons for pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can add Lucide icon names in page frontmatter and folder metadata.

faq.question: Does it run commands or install packages?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill files contain documentation guidance and examples only.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no executable code, no credential access, and no data exfiltration behavior.
